user_outcome: An operator with the Codex CLI can dispatch loop roles through a codex exec subprocess driver exactly as they can with claude-cli — the N=2 proof that the Driver contract is not claude-shaped — closing the open sworn#19 deferral.
rationale: sworn#19 (S63-deferral-1) has TODO stubs at internal/model/cli.go:59-65 (newCodexCLI returns ErrDriverNotImplemented) and internal/model/provider.go:175-180; the deferral reason was 'different invocation shapes and output normalisation'. This slice implements the codex variant over S02's shared subprocess plumbing. Codex specifics: `codex exec` runs non-interactively, takes the working directory via -C/--cd (in addition to cmd.Dir), and supports JSON output for machine parsing; token usage and final-message extraction differ from claude's envelope — that normalisation delta IS the slice. The provider.go codex stub is NOT removed here (provider.go belongs to T4's touchpoints; S05 retires it when the registry mapping lands). LATE-DEFERRAL CLAUSE (agreed at planning, 2026-07-02): if the release runs long, this slice may be re-deferred with a Rule 2 card without re-grouping any track — T2 simply shortens to S02.

effort_complexity.rationale: Small file count over S02's plumbing, but the codex envelope/exit-behaviour unknowns are the substance — the reason it was deferred from S63 in the first place.

risks.description: Codex CLI envelope/flag assumptions are wrong or drift (the original S63 deferral reason).
risks.mitigation: Fake-binary fixtures encode the assumed envelope with the codex CLI version documented in codex.go; parsing degrades gracefully (missing usage -> zeros with CostSource=unknown); S10 conformance runs the same behavioural clauses against it.

risks.description: Shared subprocess.go changes for codex regress the claude driver.
risks.mitigation: Same track, sequential after S02; S02's tests run in this slice's test commands; conformance suite (S10) covers both.

acceptance_criteria.text: When Dispatch is called with Role=implementer, a codex model ID, and a valid WorktreeRoot, the driver SHALL spawn `codex exec` non-interactively with the working directory set to WorktreeRoot (cmd.Dir and the -C flag) and machine-readable output enabled, and SHALL return Status=ok with ResultText set to the final agent message and InputTokens/OutputTokens/DurationMS/ModelID populated from the CLI's reported usage where present. TestCodexDispatchImplementer proves this with a fake codex binary emitting a canned envelope.

When Dispatch is called with Role=verifier, the driver SHALL include DispatchInput.VerdictSchema in the prompt as the required output contract and SHALL populate Result.StructuredJSON with the final message when it parses as a JSON object, returning Status=error with ErrKind=protocol when it does not (fail-closed; engine owns verifier-verdict-v1 validation).

If the codex CLI exits non-zero, exceeds DispatchInput.Timeout, or the binary is missing, Dispatch SHALL return Status=error with the same ErrKind mapping as the claude driver (timeout -> transient; binary-not-found -> config; non-zero exit -> provider with stderr excerpt) — never a panic, never a half-populated ok Result. Table-driven TestCodexErrorMapping covers all three.

The codex child environment SHALL apply the same hygiene as the claude driver (GOCACHE/GOMODCACHE outside WorktreeRoot; HOME not overridden), asserted by test; when usage metadata is absent from the envelope, cost SHALL be recorded as 0 with CostSource=unknown — never fabricated.
`go test ./internal/driver/...` SHALL pass, including the S02 claude-driver tests unchanged (shared-plumbing regression guard).
